meta.name: Garlic Shrimp
meta.subtitle: Fast skillet shrimp with garlic, lemon, herbs, and olive oil (Herb & Garlic style)

source.name: ssa.cuisines.herb_garlic
notes: Canonical SSA Herb & Garlic baseline garlic shrimp.
Designed for quick weeknight execution and deterministic doneness targets (avoid overcooking).

yield.notes: Serves 4 with sides, or 2–3 over pasta/rice as a main bowl.

required.label: Large skillet
required.notes: Use a wide pan so shrimp sear rather than steam.

optional.label: Microplane/zester
optional.notes: For lemon zest and/or finely grating garlic.

ingredients.label: Large shrimp, peeled and deveined
notes: 16/20 or 21/25 count works well.
Pat dry for best sear.
If using frozen shrimp, thaw fully and dry thoroughly.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: Adjust based on whether you finish with salty butter or cheese.

ingredients.label: Unsalted butter (optional but recommended)
notes: Adds richness and helps carry garlic flavor.
For dairy-free, omit and add 1 tbsp extra olive oil.

ingredients.label: Garlic, minced or grated
notes: Grated garlic disperses quickly for strong flavor.
Avoid burning: garlic goes in after the shrimp sears.

ingredients.label: Lemon (zest + juice)
notes: Use half for cooking and half for finishing, to keep the flavor bright.

ingredients.label: Fresh parsley, chopped
notes: Flat-leaf parsley recommended.

ingredients.label: Fresh thyme leaves (optional)
notes: Optional Herb & Garlic layer; use lightly.

ingredients.label: Dry white wine (optional) or broth
notes: Adds restaurant-style pan sauce. Substitute with chicken/seafood broth.

steps.title: Prep and season shrimp
instructions: Pat shrimp very dry with paper towels (this prevents steaming).
Season shrimp with salt and black pepper.
Zest the lemon (optional but recommended) and set aside. Cut lemon and squeeze about half the juice into a small bowl (save the rest for finishing).
checks: Shrimp is dry to the touch

steps.title: Sear shrimp quickly
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add olive oil (and butter if using).
When the fat is hot and shimmering, add shrimp in a single layer.
Cook 60–90 seconds on the first side until the edges turn pink and the bottom gets light browning.
Flip and cook 45–75 seconds more. Shrimp should be just opaque and curled into a 'C' shape (not tight 'O').
Transfer shrimp to a plate immediately to avoid overcooking.
checks: Shrimp is mostly opaque with slight translucence in center (will finish in sauce)

steps.title: Build garlic-lemon-herb sauce
instructions: Add garlic (and red pepper flakes if using) to the pan and stir 20–30 seconds until fragrant (do not brown).
Optional: deglaze with white wine/broth and simmer 30–60 seconds to reduce slightly.
Add lemon juice (the first half) and a pinch of lemon zest if using.

steps.title: Return shrimp and finish
instructions: Return shrimp (and any juices) to the pan and toss 30–45 seconds to coat and finish cooking.
Turn off heat. Stir in parsley (and thyme if using).
Squeeze remaining lemon juice to taste for brightness and adjust salt/pepper if needed.

refrigerator.guidance: Best fresh. If storing, cool quickly and refrigerate airtight. Reheat gently to avoid rubbery shrimp.

best: Skillet on low with a splash of water/broth, just until warmed
Microwave 30–45 seconds at 50% power, stirring once
avoid: High heat or long reheat (overcooks shrimp)

batchNotes: Prep garlic, herbs, and lemon before cooking; the cook happens fast.
Scale by using two skillets if doubling to prevent steaming.
leftovers: Chop and toss into a cold pasta salad with lemon and herbs
Add to rice bowls with roasted vegetables

shrimp.visual: Opaque with gentle 'C' curl, not tight 'O'

summary: Fast Herb & Garlic garlic shrimp: quick sear, then a bright garlic-lemon-herb pan sauce. Weeknight-ready in 15 minutes.

handling: Keep shrimp cold until cooking; do not leave at room temperature longer than 30 minutes.
Cook shrimp promptly after thawing; avoid cross-contamination from raw seafood.
Reheat leftovers gently and briefly to prevent overcooking.

notes: Omit butter; increase olive oil by 1 tbsp for a silky pan sauce.
Finish with extra lemon zest and parsley for brightness.
Increase red pepper flakes to 1/2 tsp.
Add a sliced chili (optional) during garlic bloom.
After deglazing, add 1/3 cup heavy cream and simmer 1–2 minutes to thicken.
Return shrimp briefly to warm through.
Toss shrimp with 1 tbsp oil, salt, pepper; air fry 400°F for 6–8 minutes, shaking halfway.
Warm garlic + oil + lemon in a pan 30 seconds, then toss cooked shrimp with herbs and lemon to finish.
